steps.title: Identify the key numerical contrast
steps.content: The passage compares consonant counts for Polynesian languages (9 in Sikaiana, 8 in ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi) against the global median (over 22), establishing they fall far below average.

steps.title: Choose the word for lowest quantity
steps.content:
”Minimal” means “smallest in degree,” which fits languages with 8–9 consonants being far below the 22+ median.

Static concerns unchanging qualities, replicable refers to reproducibility, and melodic describes sound character—none capture the numerical comparison.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
